meta.title: Olin Cycling Experiences — Tour de France 2026
meta.description: An exclusive six-day cycling experience in the French Alps around the Tour de France 2026. July 21–26. For ten riders only.

hero.intro: Welcome to a cycling experience of a lifetime.
hero.subtitle: French Alps — Tour de France 2026 — July 21–26

philosophy.label: Philosophy
philosophy.title: Olin
philosophy.subtitle: Cycling Experiences
body: Olin is a Nahuatl word. It does not simply mean movement.
It means purposeful movement. The kind that creates transformation. The kind that carries intention.
In the Aztec cosmological tradition, Olin is movement as a cosmic principle: the force that animates the universe and the act of moving forward through the world with purpose.
Olin is also the root of the Nahuatl word for heart, yollotl. Because the heart does not simply beat. It moves. It transforms. It propels.
That is what we believe cycling can be.

tourBanner.title:
Come to enjoy
the Tour with us

intro.title: Six days
body: From July 21 to 26, 2026, a small group of up to ten cyclists will gather in the French Alps for six days of riding, racing, and shared experience built around the Tour de France. We would like for you to be one of us.
This is not a large-group tour.
It is not a package holiday. It is a carefully curated week for people who love cycling, love travel, and want to experience the Tour de France the way it deserves to be experienced, from the inside.

chalet.title: Le Chalet d'Oz
chalet.summary: A private wooden chalet at the heart of the Oisans massif, minutes from Alpe d'Huez and the stage routes of TDF 2026. Five nights, bedrooms for 2–3, hot tub and sauna for recovery.

included.title: Everything that's included
included.intro: Six things we take care of so you can ride, watch the Tour, and forget about the rest.

features.title: The Tour
features.body: Live viewing of Stages 18, 19 and 20 — including the bends of Alpe d'Huez.

features.title: The Chalet
features.body: Five nights in a private chalet in Oz. Daily breakfast and dinner. Hot tub each evening.

features.title: Riding
features.body: Three guided rides with GPX files, on-road mechanic support, daily nutrition and hydration. Carbon bike rental on request ($500).

features.title: Guides & Coaching
features.body: Multilingual guides (EN / ES / FR / IT). Daily briefings, technique, pacing, and climbing coaching.

features.title: Transfers
features.body: Private van pickup from Lyon on July 21 and return drop-off on July 26.

features.title: Gear & Care
features.body: Olin founding member jersey. After-ride bike check-ups with Wolf Tooth & Park Tool kit.

items: Live viewing of TDF Stages 18, 19, and 20
Six days / five nights in the private chalet in Oz, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes (July 21–26)
Daily breakfast and dinner, including the welcome and farewell dinners
Private van transfers: Lyon pickup July 21 (9:00 am) and return drop-off July 26
Full mechanic support on ride days — support van, spares, and on-road assistance
Chalet hot tub available each evening for recovery and relaxation
Multilingual guide throughout the week (Spanish, English, French & Italian)
Daily itinerary briefings and cycling coaching on technique, pacing, and climbing
3 guided rides with GPX files provided
On-ride snacks, energy nutrition, and hydration on all cycling days
Carbon bike rental if requested (rental fee USD $500). You can bring your bike too
After rides bike check-ups with WolfTooth & Park Tools Kit
included.note: Oz is a mountain village set in the heart of the Oisans massif, minutes from Alpe d'Huez and the routes of stages 18, 19 and 20 of the world's greatest cycling race. Accommodation will be shared in bedrooms between 2 or 3 people. If interested in a private bedroom, availability is available with an additional fee.
included.perk: Also all our ten founding members will get thirty percent discount for TDF 2027 Olin experience.
included.excluded: Not included: International flights or trains to Lyon. Lunches on race-viewing days (local cafés and food stalls along the route).

days.label: Arrival & first ride
days.body: Your journey begins in Lyon — a city served by international flights and a direct TGV from Paris Charles de Gaulle in under two hours. We pick you up at 9:00 am and drive you to the chalet. The afternoon begins with a gentle introductory ride through the alpine valley: a chance to move your legs, take in the landscape, and find your rhythm with the group. In the evening, your guides walk you through the week ahead over dinner.

days.label: The big ride
days.body: A longer, more demanding day in the saddle. We take on the classic climbs of the Oisans massif — the cols that the professional peloton will ride days later. Your guides lead the way. The support van follows with mechanical assistance, snacks, and hydration. Coaching on pacing and climbing technique is woven into the ride. The hot tub waits at the chalet when you return.

days.label: Stage 18 live
days.body: The first of three consecutive days watching the Tour de France in person. We position the group at a strategic point along Stage 18's route for maximum atmosphere and proximity to the peloton. An optional light ride on a section of the stage route in the morning lets you warm up before the race arrives. Your guide provides tactical race commentary throughout.

days.label: Alpe d'Huez & Stage 19
days.body: The centerpiece of the week. We ride the 21 hairpin bends of Alpe d'Huez — one of the most celebrated climbs in all of professional cycling, a mountain that holds a place in the sport's mythology unlike any other. We ascend ahead of the race, reach the summit, and take our position to watch Stage 19 finish live around us. Few places in sport produce this kind of atmosphere.

days.label: Stage 20 & farewell dinner
days.body: A full day dedicated to Stage 20 — one of the decisive mountain stages of the 2026 Tour. We hold viewing position, guided by your team who know where the race comes alive. In the evening we return to the chalet for the farewell dinner: a celebration of the week, the rides, and the people you shared them with.

days.label: Departure
days.body: After a final breakfast together at the chalet, we drive you back to Lyon. From there you connect onward to wherever the world takes you next — carrying something the mountains tend to leave behind.

whoFor.title: Who this is for
body: Olin is built for cyclists who want more than scenery. You do not need to be a racer.
You need to love being on a bike, love mountains, and want to be inside the greatest cycling event in the world — not watching it on a screen.
Rides are structured to work for a range of abilities. Guides adapt routes and pacing to the group. If you can handle a full day in the saddle in mountain terrain, you belong here.
We are not trying to build something large. We are trying to build something unforgettable.

Olin travels by word of mouth. There is no public listing, no advertising, and no waiting list. When ten people have confirmed, the group is complete. Comparable curated cycling weeks in the Alps range from $4,500 to $7,000.
pricing.priceLabel: This is your founding member price.
pricing.priceSub: This price is deliberately lower than what Olin will charge at full launch. You are being invited as a founding member — one of the first ten people to experience this, before it exists in its final form.
pricing.shape: You help shape what this becomes.
pricing.shapeBody: After the trip, we will sit down — in person or over a call — and ask you what worked, what didn't, and what would have made it better.
pricing.deposit: To secure your place, a deposit of USD $1,700 is required. This is deducted from the total price and is fully refundable up to 60 days before departure. It confirms your spot and holds it until the group is complete.
pricing.reply: Reply to the person who shared this invitation with you, or reach out directly to confirm your interest. We will send you the next steps.

about.title: About us
body: Olin is led by Augusto and Rodrigo — two riders who have spent years in the saddle across France, Italy, Spain, the UK, the United States, Singapore, Japan, and Latin America.
Augusto has personally guided groups through the Tour de France, the Vuelta a España, and the Giro d'Italia.

Rodrigo brings nearly a decade as an Airbnb Superhost, a certified bicycle technician through the Bicycle Industry Employers Association, and a Rapha member who has visited flagship stores across the world.

Together they raced criteriums in New York City on teams that held local jersey titles. They have not built Olin as a business idea. They built it because it is the trip they always wanted to take — and now they are inviting ten people to take it with them.

quote.text: Nahui Olin. The age of purposeful movement. The heart that moves, transforms.

footer.tagline: French Alps — Tour de France 2026 — July 21–26

book.name: Full name
book.jerseyHint: Free text — write your size, brand fit, or chest measurement. Each founding member receives an Olin jersey.
book.depositNote: Your deposit is fully refundable up to 60 days before departure.

consent.title: Before you reserve, please confirm:
consent.waiver: I have read and agree to the <link>Participant Waiver</link>, including the Assumption of Risk and Release of Liability. I confirm I am over 18 and physically fit to participate.
consent.insurance: I confirm I hold, or will obtain before departure, valid travel insurance covering cycling activities in France, including emergency medical evacuation.
consent.privacy: I agree to the <link>Privacy Policy</link> and consent to Olin processing my booking data (name, contact details, emergency contact, medical notes) for the purpose of delivering this experience.
consent.cancellation: I have read and agree to the <link>Cancellation & Refund Policy</link>.
consent.optionalIntro: Optional — you can change your mind anytime by emailing info@olin.bike:
consent.feedback: I consent to Olin contacting me after the trip for feedback (one short call or written survey, no marketing).
consent.media: I consent to Olin using photos and video that include me, captured during the trip, for promotional purposes (website, social media, future invitations). I can withdraw this consent at any time.
consent.helperHint: Please tick all required boxes above to enable payment.

confirmation.title: You're in.
confirmation.subtitle: Your place is confirmed
confirmation.body: We've received your deposit. You'll receive a confirmation email shortly with next steps — including your pre-trip questionnaire.
